name: Bit Crusher
description: This algorithm is an implementation of the disting mk4 algorithm of the same name. It applies sample rate and sample depth reduction to deliberately introduce quantisation and aliasing artefacts. It also optionally applies bitwise modification of the samples for non-linear distortion effects.
short_description: Sample rate and bit depth reduction
gui_description: Bit Crusher algorithm interface with bit reduction and mangling controls

parameters.name: Bit reduce
parameters.description: Sets the amount of bit reduction.

parameters.name: Symmetric
parameters.description: Chooses symmetric or asymmetric bit reduction.

parameters.name: Downsample
parameters.description: Controls the downsampling (sample rate reduction), scaled at 0.8V/octave.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of busses to process, starting from the Input. For example, for a stereo signal, set this to 2.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

input_ports.name: Input
input_ports.description: The first input bus to process.

input_ports.name: Downsample input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the downsample CV, which is added to the Downsample parameter value. (This corresponds to the Y input on the disting mk4 algorithm.)

input_ports.name: Input
input_ports.description: The first input bus to process.

input_ports.name: Downsample input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the downsample CV, which is added to the Downsample parameter value. (This corresponds to the Y input on the disting mk4 algorithm.)
